text.content:
Sandbox layer for Loftwick. Use these pages to give individual PCs something to do during downtime, when the active investigation only needs two or three of them. Each PC has an anchor location and an anchor NPC; the city has a civic calendar, carousing and information-gathering tables, training and crafting references, and a small library of sub-team mini-quests. Nothing here advances the conspiracy. Everything here gives the table texture between investigation beats.

What's in this journal

  • Per-PC anchors — one location and one NPC per PC, where they are known and welcome.
  • Civic calendar — what's happening in Loftwick week by week. Festivals, market days, council sessions, militia drill days, religious observances.
  • Carousing & information-gathering — d10 tables for what a PC might pick up over an evening at a tavern, the market, a temple, a militia yard, a salon.
  • Crafting, training, recuperation — reference matrix of what Loftwick offers, who provides it, and at what cost in days and gold.
  • Sub-team mini-quests — four short scenes that resolve in a single evening. Optional. Pure character moments, no conspiracy advancement.

How to use these pages

The party returns to Loftwick in Phase 1 (the welcome scenes), Phase 2 (the investigation of Haskur's gear and the political pressure), and Phase 5 (the aftermath). Each of those phases has slow days where two or three PCs are tied up in active scenes — a debrief with Caelith, a courier-chain stakeout, an audience with Brane — while the others are not. That is the moment to reach for these pages.

The anchor NPCs do not advance the conspiracy. They have their own small problems, their own small expertise, and their own small reasons to be glad when one of the PCs walks through their door. None of them will be killed by the conspiracy as a Pressure Clock advancement. They are deliberately safe ground.

Each PC has a place they are known in Loftwick and a person who is glad when they walk in. These are not romantic relationships unless the table wants them to be; they are the kind of friendly professional-and-personal connections that working adventurers accumulate over years of returning to a city.

Cam — the halfling rogue

Anchor location: Old Mira's Pawn-and-Borrow, on Wick Lane two doors down from the stationer's where Merro Gilst rented his rooms. A narrow shopfront in dark walnut with a brass weight-scale in the window. Locked at night by a deadbolt only Cam and three other people can pick.

Anchor NPC: Mistress Mira Welk, Loftwick-born half-orc, sixties, gone grey at the temples, still strong in the shoulders. Runs the pawnshop legitimately and the discreet receiver-of-goods side-business beneath it. Has known Cam since he was twenty and has bought-and-sold for him three times — once at a loss to her, twice at a loss to him, which is the way a long professional friendship is balanced. Calls him halfling cousin in private (her mother was a halfling; she is built like neither parent).

What she offers: Fence-rate buy-and-sell on stolen goods (60% of value, no questions). Short-term loans against pawned items (she keeps things for six weeks before they go on the public shelf). Rumor on which Loftwick discount-house is calling its loans this season. A back room with a kettle and two chairs where Cam can sit out the rain.

What she needs (if Cam offers): A persistent shoplifter has been hitting her on Brewfest weekends. She would like the problem identified and dissuaded; she does not want them hurt or arrested.

Elle — the halfling monk

Anchor location: The Cloister of the Open Way, a small monastic chapter house on the eastern edge of the Civic Way, attached to a working temple of Phaulkon (god of the open sky). A stone-walled courtyard with a sand-floored sparring circle, a small dormitory, and a refectory that smells of barley and onions all winter.

Anchor NPC: Brother Ashan Vell, a stout middle-aged human monk in his fifties, Yeomanry-born, formerly a militia drill-sergeant before he took the Open Way at thirty-two. Bald, broad-shouldered, slow-spoken, and one of the few sparring partners who can match Elle's halfling speed by dropping his own size and weight against it. Calls her small sister of the wind.

What he offers: Sparring (any morning between Lauds and the third bell). Use of the dormitory if she wants quiet sleep. Access to the chapter library (small but thoughtfully curated; includes two volumes of pre-Aerdy monastic writing she would not find elsewhere). Bread and barley-stew in the refectory.

What he needs (if Elle offers): One of his junior brothers has been losing his nerve in sparring. Brother Ashan would like Elle to spar with the boy gently for an evening — "a small sister can teach a young brother things a big brother cannot."

Kitty — the chthonic tiefling druid

Anchor location: The Reedrush, a small wetland a mile southwest of Loftwick's South Gate, where the Sheldomar's slowest tributary widens before it becomes farmland. A standing willow grove, a low stone bench almost lost in the reeds, the soft press of birds Kitty does not always see clearly.

Anchor NPC: Mother Felun, an old hedge-druid (human, Flan, late seventies, weathered to driftwood), who lives in a thatched cottage on the eastern edge of the Reedrush. Wears a brown wool dress and a single necklace of small bones (rabbit, otter, kingfisher, all gathered, never killed). Speaks Common, Old Faith Druidic, and a little Suloise. Knows Kitty's chthonic ancestry on sight and is not afraid of it; Felun has met older things and remembers them with respect rather than alarm.

What she offers: Quiet. Tea brewed from herbs Kitty does not recognize. Conversation about the small druidic work of the Reedrush — which trees are tired this year, where the otters have moved their den, what the marsh-harriers are fighting about. Three nights of safe lodging if Kitty needs to be away from the city. The use of a small private spring where Kitty can leave offerings the city would not respect.

What she needs (if Kitty offers): A pair of city-children have been throwing stones at the marsh-harriers from the South Gate causeway. Felun would like the children identified and spoken to — by Kitty, not by Felun, because Kitty is the one whose presence will be remembered.

Gianni — the human ranger

Anchor location: The Southwall Yard, the militia archers' practice yard inside the south barracks. Sand-floored, with three firing lanes against straw butts, a small armory, and a covered loft where Gianni can wait out the rain.

Anchor NPC: Sergeant Doral Hurst, an old militia archer (human, Yeomanry, late fifties), retired from the southern district campaigns after a knee that no longer holds him in a long march. Now runs the city archers' drill three mornings a week. Grey-bearded, dry-humored, and respects Gianni's woodcraft enough to ask her opinion on tracks at the South Gate's outer perimeter when he is not too proud.

What he offers: Use of the firing lanes whenever the yard is open. Sparring with the practice longbow against his quiet professional eye. Coffee from the small brazier in his office. The chance to read whatever new militia field manual the Yeomanry has issued (he keeps copies). One reliable name in any of the southern districts — Doral has friends in eight gates of nine.

What he needs (if Gianni offers): A militia recruit has been finding her sights pulled to the right by a half-inch, every shot. Doral has not been able to see the cause. He suspects a hand injury the recruit is hiding. He would like Gianni to spar a few rounds with her and confirm.

Selvara — the human sorcerer

Anchor location: Master Ferrick's Reading Room, the back of a small bookbinder's shop on the Civic Way's quieter end. A single dark-walnut reading table, four chairs, a small private hearth, and a wall of bound volumes on Aerdy and pre-Aerdy magical theory.

Anchor NPC: Master Olen Ferrick, a retired Yeomanry academician (human, late sixties, bald, with a single thick grey beard he pulls when thinking). Was a reader of magical theory at the Audit Hall's old academy before the academy folded its arcane chair forty years ago. Now keeps a private library and corresponds with a small fellowship of Yeomanry sorcerers and one Aerdy magister. Recognizes Selvara as a sorcerer by the faint pre-light signature she carries; will not speak of it openly but will speak of it.

What he offers: Free use of the reading room (Selvara has her own key after the second visit). Access to his correspondence library — particularly two volumes she will not find elsewhere on the theory of inherited (rather than studied) sorcery. Tea, cold pressed apple, and an old man's careful conversation about the difference between magic that is taught and magic that is born. Three nights of quiet lodging in a small back room if Selvara needs to be away from the inn.

What he needs (if Selvara offers): One of his correspondents in the southern districts has gone silent for the third month. Master Ferrick has written; the correspondent has not replied. He would like Selvara, when next she rides south on the party's business, to detour by the correspondent's address and confirm that the silence is age and not trouble.

Alicia — the warlock

Anchor: Trina Alvere remains Alicia's primary anchor in Loftwick — established in Phase 2 and continuing through every subsequent phase. See Trina Alvere — Profile and Mentor Notes for Alicia in Phase 2 — Journals. Trina is for Alicia what Master Ferrick is for Selvara, what Mother Felun is for Kitty: the patient, knowing, slightly-distant senior who sees what Alicia is and does not flinch from it. The anchor is intact.

What Trina offers (during downtime specifically): Use of her private library; quiet sparring talk about Pact-of-the-Blade theory; the chance to read entries in a specific bound volume Trina keeps on a high shelf and lends only to Alicia. Trina also serves as the one person in Loftwick to whom Alicia can speak openly about the pact's small difficult moments without alarm.

pages.name: Civic Calendar — What's Happening in Loftwick This Week

text.content:
The Yeomanry runs on a deliberate civic rhythm. Council sessions, market days, militia drills, and religious observances stack predictably across the week and the year. The party arrives at different points in this rhythm in each phase; the calendar here gives the GM a quick reference so the city feels like it is on a schedule the party is interrupting, not a stage set up for them.

The Yeomanry year

Greyhawk's standard twelve-month calendar applies. Four festivals divide the year into seasonal quarters:

  • Needfest (midwinter, between Sunsebb and Fireseek). The civic year's pause. Council does not sit. Markets are closed for three days at the heart of the festival. Temples hold their largest observances of the year. Militia drill is suspended. Most adventuring parties returning to Loftwick during Needfest find the city quiet, slow, and unexpectedly hospitable; people invite strangers in for bread and small beer.
  • Growfest (spring, between Coldeven and Planting). The civic year resumes. Markets reopen with the first imported southern goods. The militia drill expands to half-strength again. Councils resume.
  • Richfest (midsummer, between Wealsun and Reaping). The Yeomanry's celebration of the working year. Markets are at peak. Militia drill is at full strength. Three days of public games (foot-races, archery competitions, halfling-and-human wrestling). The Audit Hall hosts a public lecture series.
  • Brewfest (autumn, between Patchwall and Ready'reat). Harvest. Markets are at peak again, with the year's grain coming in. Three days of public drinking and recovery. Pickpockets are at their busiest; Cam's anchor Mistress Welk is at hers.

The Loftwick week

DayWhat's openNotes
StardayMarkets (full); Council not in session; militia drill 6:30–9:00The week's largest market day. The Civic Way is crowded from sunrise to mid-afternoon.
SundayTemples (full); markets (small); militia closedReligious observance day. Temple of Phaulkon (Elle's anchor) holds its weekly noon service. Other temples follow their own rhythms.
MoondayCouncil in session; markets (small); militia drill 6:30–9:00Council session day. Brane and the closure faction are at the chamber from the second bell. Audit Hall is at full activity.
GodsdayMarkets (full); Council in session morning only; militia drill 6:30–9:00Second main market day. Council meets briefly to certify the prior week's votes and adjourn for committee work.
WaterdayMarkets (small); Council not in session; militia drill 6:30–9:00The quiet middle of the week. Best day for private audiences; the Council corridors are nearly empty.
EarthdayMarkets (small); Council in session afternoon only; militia drill 6:30–9:00Committee day. Specialty trades hold their guild meetings (the cooper's guild meets at the third bell, the bookbinder's at the seventh).
FreedayMarkets (medium); Council not in session; militia drill closedThe week's working-rest day. Many shops close at the seventh bell; taverns at their busiest from the seventh through the second bell of the next morning.

What's in season during each phase

  • Phase 1 (party returns from defeating Haskur Vandrell): autumn, between Brewfest and Needfest. Markets are still in their post-Brewfest recovery; the civic year is winding toward midwinter pause. Council sessions run shorter than usual.
  • Phase 2 (the dead man's receipts): same season, the week or two after Phase 1. Civic rhythm unchanged.
  • Phase 5 (the small matter): mid-spring, between Growfest and Richfest. Markets reopened, militia at full drill, civic life at its springtime peak. The Closure motion's binding vote falls on a Council session day (Day 9 of the phase = Moonday, by the calendar). Plan the Pressure Clock segments around this.

Local events the party may run into

  • The Cooper's Guild Meet (Earthday at the third bell). Cooper's Hall on Cooper's Lane. Open to the public during the first hour. A good place to overhear merchant gossip about the docks and the river trade.
  • The Bookbinder's Guild Meet (Earthday at the seventh bell). Bookbinder's Hall on the Civic Way. Open during the first half-hour. Master Ferrick (Selvara's anchor) attends most weeks. A good place for Selvara to read off a list of recent imported titles.
  • The Phaulkon morning service (Sunday at the second bell after dawn). Temple of Phaulkon on the Civic Way's eastern end. Brother Ashan (Elle's anchor) attends. Quiet, brief, full of the sound of unkennelled hawks circling the courtyard above the worshipers.
  • The Reedrush morning (any day at first light). The Reedrush wetland southwest of the South Gate. Mother Felun (Kitty's anchor) is most easily found at this hour. The marsh-harriers are loudest at first light.
  • The Southwall morning drill (Starday, Moonday, Godsday, Waterday, Earthday at the second bell after dawn). South Barracks. Sergeant Hurst (Gianni's anchor) is on the firing lanes. Gianni is welcome.

A PC who spends an evening (or a morning, or a long afternoon) in one of the city's social spaces may pick up something useful, something idle, or something the GM can develop into a future thread. The d10 tables below give the GM a roll-and-pick option; pick by hand if a specific result fits better.

An evening at a Loftwick tavern

Cost: 5 sp for a passable evening (modest fare, two ales, a bench by the fire). 1 gp for a comfortable one (better fare, three ales, a chair near the fire). 5 gp for a memorable one (a private booth, the night's best wine, the kitchen's best dish, and the loyalty of the serving-girl for the rest of the visit).

Roll Persuasion or Insight DC 12 to make the evening yield. On success, roll d10 below.

d10What you heard
1The Audit Hall has been short three clerks for two weeks. Nobody knows why. The clerk-pool is talking.
2A new wool-trader from Hardby is buying small lots above the going price. He is paying in silver, not credit. Nobody knows him.
3A halfling cousin of the speaker (the speaker is always human) has just opened a new pawn-and-borrow off Wick Lane and is undercutting Old Mira's prices. The speaker thinks Mira is going to be unhappy. (False — there is no new pawnshop. The speaker is mistaken.)
4The Phaulkon temple's roof has had a slow leak all winter. The chapter is putting up a small public collection.
5A militia corporal at the South Gate was seen drinking at the Brass Lantern with a man whose accent was Hardby-aligned. Nobody else thinks much of it. (Worth noting; not necessarily significant.)
6The serving-girl's mother is the cook at Master Ferrick's bookbinder's. The girl will pass a note for a copper.
7The Brewfest pickpocket gangs are working the Civic Way more boldly than usual this autumn. Three watch reports in two days.
8A southern-district recruit at the South Barracks has been crying at night. The bunk-sergeant is at his wits' end. (Hook for Gianni's mini-quest 1.)
9The Audit Hall's foreign-correspondence office has a new rumor about the Vandrell file. Nobody can quite say what the rumor is. The rumor is itself the news. (In Phase 5: this is the conspiracy testing the broadside attack water.)
10A man at the next table is buying ale for everyone and is talking very fast about a road he rode last Brewfest from Hardby to Loftwick that he says was haunted. He is drunk. The road is not haunted. He saw something.

A morning at the Civic Way market

Cost: free, plus 2-5 sp for purchases. Investigation or Persuasion DC 11 to make the morning yield. On success, roll d10.

d10What you saw or heard
1A Hardby wool-merchant is buying eight bales over the going price. (See tavern result 2.)
2The grain-and-meat row is a copper short on the southern-district price. The southern districts are paying in militia scrip — bandit pressure on the trade road has cut their cash.
3A small boy has lost a copper. He is crying. A halfling stranger gives him two coppers and walks on without speaking.
4The fishmonger at the second stall has the year's best smoked pike. Worth 8 sp for an evening's eating; ask the GM if the PC's class would notice.
5A herald reads a Council bulletin at the third bell. (In Phase 5: it is the Closure motion's public-record entry. In other phases: routine civic notices.)
6An old Flan woman is selling small bone necklaces from a basket. Mother Felun is in the city today. She is known to Kitty.
7The bookbinder's apprentice is on errand at the herb-stall. He recognizes Selvara if she has visited Master Ferrick's reading room; he nods.
8A militia recruit is sitting on the steps of the meat-and-grain row, eating a small bread, looking deeply tired. (Hook for Gianni's mini-quest 1.)
9The herald's bulletin includes a small mention of a fence's pawnshop being investigated for receiving stolen goods. The fence is not Mistress Welk. Cam will recognize the name.
10A Hardby-coined silver tower-and-three-waves passes through the bread-stall's till. The baker accepts it without comment. The party may purchase it back from her for 2 sp; it is the same countermark on Haskur's slug.

An afternoon at a Loftwick salon

Salons in Loftwick are smaller and more academic than Hardby's. Two main hosts: Trina Alvere (Alicia's mentor; her salons are by invitation, third Earthday of each month) and Master Olen Ferrick (Selvara's anchor; his are open to invited friends, second Waterday of each month). Outside those, a private salon may be arranged by request through the Audit Hall's social secretary (4 gp registration fee, two weeks' notice).

Cost: free if invited, 4 gp registration if arranging. Persuasion or History DC 13 to make the afternoon yield. On success, roll d10.

d10What you learned
1A Yeomanry scholar has just published a small monograph on pre-Aerdy paleography. (Of interest to Selvara and Alicia.)
2The Audit Hall's foreign-inquiry budget reduction is being whispered about in academic circles. (In Phase 5: this is the Closure motion's private-knowledge phase. In other phases: routine budget gossip.)
3A junior bookbinder has hand-bound a small folio of Yeomanry militia field songs. Trina has a copy. She lends it freely.
4A retired southern-district magistrate (now a Yeomanry historian) is writing a book about the bandit pressure on the Hool Marsh trade road. Edda Halvern (Phase 5 NPC) has been a source. (In Phase 5: this is your hook into Halvern's side-deal motivation.)
5An Aerdy poet is in town for the season, lodging at the Brass Lantern. He has, by all accounts, a very good ear and a very small purse.
6Trina mentions, casually, a correspondent of hers in the wood. She does not name them. (In Phase 5: this is the Aldea hook landing early. In other phases: foreshadowing.)
7An academic argument has erupted over the precedence of pre-Aerdy versus pre-Suel versus pre-Flan paleography. Master Ferrick has his views. They are not shy.
8A Yeomanry sorcerer-of-modest-record has been visiting Master Ferrick's reading room more often than usual. Selvara may meet him there if she chooses. (Optional NPC for Selvara: a sorcerer rival, friendly or otherwise.)
9The Audit Hall's lost three clerks (see tavern result 1) are rumored to have left for Hardby in the same week. Two went south, one east. Nobody knows why. The salon is uneasy.
10Trina passes the PC, on her way out, a small folded note that simply says: "Walk south of the city tomorrow morning. The Reedrush is interesting today." (Hook for Kitty's mini-quest 1, or for any PC who acts on it.)

What Loftwick offers a PC who has time to spend on improving themselves, their gear, or their health. Costs and times below are baseline; adjust by class affinity, anchor-NPC favor, or campaign economy.

Crafting and gear

ServiceProviderCost / TimeNotes
Mundane weapon repairHesp Veld, the Civic Way smith5 sp / 1 dayWorks while you wait if simple. Knows ranger archery work and will sharpen Gianni's broadheads at no extra cost.
Custom weapon commissionHesp Veldfull PHB price + 20% / 3-7 daysOne-handed weapons are his specialty. Two-handed by request; takes longer.
Armor repairMara Olest, harness-maker, two doors south of Hesp's1 gp / 2 daysWill adjust halfling-fitted armor for Cam and Elle without remark; some smaller smiths will not.
Studded leather custom-fitMara Olest50 gp / 7 daysCam's preferred armor; she has his measurements on file from a prior visit.
Bow repair / restringingOld Tellis at the Southwall Yard (Sergeant Hurst introduces)2 sp / same dayTellis is the militia's bowyer; will work for an outsider only on Hurst's word.
Spell-component refreshMaster Ferrick's reading room1 gp + components at cost / 1 dayMaster Ferrick keeps a small store of common components; rare components must be ordered from Hardby (3 weeks).
Druidic focus repair / new focusMother Felun at the Reedrushfree for Kitty / 1-3 daysFelun will not accept payment from a fellow practitioner; a small offering left at the spring is appropriate.
Identifying a magic item (cantrip-grade common)Master Ferrick5 gp / 1 dayIdentifies common magic items reliably; uncommon items in 2-3 days; rare items beyond his expertise — refer to a Hardby specialist.
Identifying an enchantment of unknown gradeTrina Alvere (Alicia only, by request)free for Alicia / 1 dayTrina identifies any enchantment Alicia brings her, including ones Master Ferrick cannot. Limit: one item per visit; Trina will not analyze more than is necessary.

Training

The 2024 Downtime Training rules apply. A PC may spend 250 days and 250 gp to learn a new language or proficiency. Loftwick offers all of the following at the standard rate; the trainer column lists the appropriate anchor or contact.

Skill / ToolTrainerNotes
Old Aerdi (language)Master FerrickReads it natively; trains it slowly.
Suloise (language)Master FerrickLess fluent than in Old Aerdi; capable trainer.
Druidic (language)Mother Felun (only for druids and Kitty's class)Felun will not teach Druidic to a non-practitioner.
Yeomanry diplomatic cipher (writing)The Audit Hall (any clerk willing; Caelith's office in Phase 1-2)Trainable; rare for outsiders to be taught.
Tinker's tools / leatherworker's tools / bowyer's toolsMara Olest (leather), Old Tellis (bow), the cooper's guild (tinker)Standard rate for all three.
Insight (skill)Brother Ashan at the Cloister (any PC)The Open Way teaches reading-of-rooms as part of basic discipline.
Stealth (skill, halfling-applicable)Mistress Welk (Cam only; she will not teach a non-trusted PC)Cam may teach to others under Welk's tolerance.
Athletics (skill)Sergeant Hurst at the Southwall YardStandard rate; militia drill itself counts as half-rate training.
Arcana (skill)Master Ferrick or Trina AlvereTrina is the deeper teacher but accepts only Alicia and (rarely) Selvara.

Recuperation

Per the 2024 Recuperation rules, three days of rest at modest cost restores all hit point maximums and ends one disease or one persistent effect. Loftwick offers the following:

ServiceProviderCost / DayNotes
Modest inn (Brass Lantern, Merchant's Rest, similar)multiple2-5 gp / dayStandard recuperation. Three days fully restores.
Quiet lodging at the Cloister of the Open WayBrother Ashan (Elle only, free; others by Brother Ashan's invitation, 1 gp donation)1 gp / dayAdds a small bonus: the recuperator may end two persistent effects rather than one (the Open Way teaches breathing forms that accelerate recovery).
The Reedrush (camping)Mother Felun's tolerance (Kitty free; others by Felun's invitation)freeRecovery in the marsh adds a small druidic restorative; the recuperator wakes with one Inspiration die after the third day.
Master Ferrick's back roomMaster Ferrick (Selvara free; others by his invitation)2 gp / dayQuiet, scholarly. Includes use of the reading room.
The Temple of Phaulkon's pilgrim cellsBrother Ashan's chapter (any PC, donation 5 sp - 2 gp)variesOpen to all pilgrims; expects a donation, which need not be large.
Cure disease (clerical)Temple of Phaulkon, by donation50 gp standardHigher-grade illnesses (mummy rot, lycanthropy, etc.) may require referral to a more specialized temple in Hardby or beyond.

Five short scenes that resolve in a single evening or morning of table time. Each is keyed to one or two PCs by class affinity but may be picked up by anyone the GM thinks fits. None advance the conspiracy. They give the table texture during downtime — a small story that is just about the PC and the city.

1. Cam — "The Persistent Shoplifter" (rogue affinity)

Mistress Welk has been hit on three Brewfest weekends in a row. Small thefts — a brass weight, a copper pendant, a half-pound of wax — never enough to call the watch, always enough to be infuriating. She would like the problem identified and dissuaded; she does not want anyone hurt or arrested.

The shoplifter is a girl of fourteen, southern-district orphan, who works the Brewfest crowds because the militia is stretched and the watch is hung-over. She steals to feed her younger brother, who is six and lives in a barn off Wick Lane.

How it resolves: Cam (or whichever PC takes the scene) tracks the girl across one Brewfest morning. Investigation or Insight DC 12 to follow her without being spotted. When confronted gently, she will not run; she will look at the floor. Cam may give her a small bag of coppers and a name (Mistress Welk's, who hires occasional sweeping at 1 sp/day), or pass her quietly to Brother Ashan at the Cloister, or simply let her go. Mistress Welk will be pleased with whichever resolution does not involve the watch.

Reward: Welk owes Cam a favor she will pay back in Phase 5 (a sealed letter to a Hardby fence she trusts; usable for the courier-chain investigation if Cam asks).

2. Elle — "The Brother Who Lost His Nerve" (monk affinity)

Brother Ashan asks Elle to spar a few rounds with a junior brother named Wend, fifteen years old, recently arrived from the southern districts. Wend has frozen in three consecutive sparring matches with the older brothers. Brother Ashan suspects fear is becoming habit and would like a different teacher to break the pattern.

Wend is taller than Elle by a foot and a half, and he knows it. He is afraid he will hurt her. He is more afraid he will not hurt her and the older brothers will see.

How it resolves: Elle spars three rounds with Wend. Athletics or Acrobatics DC 13 to read his fear correctly; on success, Elle understands the problem in the first round and can adjust her teaching. The right answer is to let Wend land a clean strike (a fall the floor takes for her, not a real one) and walk back to her starting position smiling. The wrong answer is to demonstrate her speed; he will fold further.

Reward: Brother Ashan grants Elle a small ceramic medallion of the Open Way that opens her access to two further volumes in the chapter library and to the Cloister at any hour, including late.

3. Kitty — "The Stone-Throwing Children" (druid affinity)

Mother Felun would like Kitty to find and speak to a pair of city-children who have been throwing stones at the Reedrush's marsh-harriers from the South Gate causeway. The children are eleven and nine, brother and sister, and the stones have not yet hit a bird; Felun would like to keep it that way.

The children are the orphaned children of a militia archer who died at the southern-district campaign two years ago and a baker's apprentice who took her own life six months later. They live with their grandmother off Cooper's Lane. They are not cruel; they are bored, and the marsh-harriers are the only thing in their lives that responds to them.

How it resolves: Kitty walks south at the second bell after dawn, finds them at the causeway, and speaks to them. Insight DC 12 to read what they actually need (Kitty's chthonic-tiefling presence is a complication; the children may be afraid of her). The right answer is to show them how to call the harriers in without throwing — a slow soft whistle Kitty teaches that the harriers will respond to. The wrong answer is to scold them and walk away; they will throw stones again, harder, the next morning.

Reward: The children adopt Kitty (loosely) and will, in any future phase the party returns to Loftwick, run errands for her at half-rate (1 sp where 2 sp is the going rate).

4. Gianni — "The Recruit With the Pulled Sights" (ranger affinity)

Sergeant Hurst would like Gianni to spar a few rounds at the Southwall Yard with a recruit named Aren, a young woman of nineteen who has been pulling her shots half an inch right for two weeks. Hurst suspects an injury Aren is hiding; he cannot get her to admit it because he is her sergeant.

Aren is left-handed and has injured the small finger of her right hand in a tavern brawl she is not supposed to have been in. The injury is mending but slowly; she has not told Hurst because she is afraid of being put on light duties and losing her place in the southern-district rotation.

How it resolves: Gianni shoots three rounds with Aren on the firing lanes. Perception or Medicine DC 13 to spot the injury. The right answer is to confirm Aren's fear (the injury is real and will heal in three weeks if rested) and offer her a private grip-correction that compensates without involving the sergeant. The wrong answer is to report it to Hurst; Aren will lose her rotation and the rest of the recruit cohort will know Gianni reported.

Reward: Aren will, in Phase 5, be the South Barracks militia trooper on the night-watch shift Marshal Thale's whistle response runs through. She will respond to Gianni's call faster than she would respond to anyone else's.

5. Selvara — "The Silent Correspondent" (sorcerer affinity)

Master Ferrick's correspondent in the southern districts has gone three months without writing back. Master Ferrick is too old to ride south himself; he would like Selvara, when next she rides through the southern-district road on the party's business, to detour by the correspondent's address and confirm that the silence is age and not trouble.

The correspondent is Mistress Reva Lodd, eighty-one, retired Yeomanry magical theorist, living alone in a small stone cottage outside the village of Two-Brooks. She is alive but failing — her hands are no longer steady enough to write, and her eyes are no longer good enough to read what Master Ferrick sends. She is not unhappy. She is simply silent.

How it resolves: Selvara visits Two-Brooks (a half-day's detour from the Loftwick-Hardby road). Insight or Medicine DC 11 to read the situation correctly — Mistress Lodd is not in trouble, she is in the long quiet end of an academic life. The right answer is to sit with her for a morning, write a letter to Master Ferrick on her behalf, and make a small arrangement with the village's miller's daughter to read Master Ferrick's letters aloud to her once a week (5 sp/week from Master Ferrick's account, which Master Ferrick will pay gladly). The wrong answer is to bring Mistress Lodd back to Loftwick; she does not want to leave Two-Brooks, and the journey will harm her.

Reward: Master Ferrick's correspondence ledger opens to Selvara — including a folded letter, in his own hand, from a Hardby sorcerer named Vell Marad who corresponds with him on pre-Aerdy theory. Useful in Phase 3 if Selvara wants a non-investigation contact in Hardby.
